title: Create Recommendation
content:
# Create Recommendation

Create a new Recommendation. The new recommendation will automatically inherit the state `pending`.

## Endpoint

`POST /recommendations/create`

code_examples.code:
from kelvin.api import Client

client = Client(api_key="your-api-key")
response = client.recommendation.createRecommendation()
print(response)

confidence.description: Confidence level of the Recommendation. This is usually, but not mandatory, related to any machine learning model confidence results.

custom_identifier.description: An optional custom identifier for any purpose.

description.example: Beam pump speed AI optimizer application recommends a new value for the speed setpoint of the controller.

expiration_date.description: UTC time when any the Recommendation will expire and the `status` automatically marked as `expired`, formatted in RFC 3339. The operator will not be able to take any further actions on this Recommendation. If no date is given, then the Recommendation will never expire.

metadata.description: Custom dictionary keys/values for use by clients for anything useful and related to the Recommendation.

resource.description: The asset that this Recommendation is related to.

trace_id.description: The Trace ID associated with the Recommendation used for tracing and debugging.

type.description: The Recommendation Type `name` associated with the Recommendation.

evidences.description: A list of evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

type.description: Type of evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

payload.description: The payload of the evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

actions.description: A list of all actions to be performed if the Recommendation is accepted.

control_changes.description: An array of objects with Control Change information. If the Recommendation is `pending`, it will display creation information or if the Recommendation is `accepted` or `auto_accepted` it will show the Control Change status. Each Control Change does not need to be related to the `resource` of the Recommendation.

control_change_id.description: Unique identifier id for the Control Change request. This will only be returned when the Recommendation state is `accepted` or `auto_accepted` where the actions have been created and an id registered on the Server.

expiration_date.description: UTC time when any the Control Change initiated will expire and the `status` automatically marked as `failed`, formatted in RFC 3339.

resource.description: The asset / data stream pair that this Control Change will be applied to.

retries.description: Number of retry attempts to write the Control Change to the Bridge until a `processed` acknowledgment is received from the Bridge. If number of attempts exceeds `retries` then the Control Change Manager updates the Control Change key `state` with the value `failed`.

timeout.description: Time in seconds the Control Change Manager will wait after sending the Control Change for the Bridge to send a `processed` acknowledgement reply before retry sending the Control Change.

type.description: The type of the action. This is used to identify the action in the system.

type_title.description: The type name of the action.

title.description: The title of the action. This is a short description of the action that will be displayed to the user.

description.example: Beam Pump GW-B12-037 needs to be diagnosed.
description.description: The description of the action. This is a longer description of the action that will be displayed to the user.

expiration_date.description: The expiration date of the action. The action will be automatically cancelled if it is not completed by this date.

payload.description: The payload is a JSON object that contains the necessary information for the executor to perform the action. The structure of the payload will depend on the action type.

confidence.description: Confidence level of the Recommendation. This is usually, but not mandatory, related to any machine learning model confidence results.

custom_identifier.description: An optional custom identifier for any purpose.

description.example: Beam pump speed AI optimizer application recommends a new value for the speed setpoint of the controller.

expiration_date.description: UTC time when any the Recommendation will expire and the `status` automatically marked as `expired`, formatted in RFC 3339. The operator will not be able to take any further actions on this Recommendation. If no date is given, then the Recommendation will never expire.

metadata.description: Custom dictionary keys/values for use by clients for anything useful and related to the Recommendation.

resource.description: The asset that this Recommendation is related to.

trace_id.description: The Trace ID associated with the Recommendation used for tracing and debugging.

type.description: The Recommendation Type `name` associated with the Recommendation.

evidences.description: A list of evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

type.description: Type of evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

payload.description: The payload of the evidence that is being used to create the Recommendation.

actions.description: A list of all actions to be performed if the Recommendation is accepted.

control_changes.description: An array of objects with Control Change information. If the Recommendation is `pending`, it will display creation information or if the Recommendation is `accepted` or `auto_accepted` it will show the Control Change status. Each Control Change does not need to be related to the `resource` of the Recommendation.

control_change_id.description: Unique identifier id for the Control Change request. This will only be returned when the Recommendation state is `accepted` or `auto_accepted` where the actions have been created and an id registered on the Server.

expiration_date.description: UTC time when any the Control Change initiated will expire and the `status` automatically marked as `failed`, formatted in RFC 3339.

resource.description: The asset / data stream pair that this Control Change will be applied to.

retries.description: Number of retry attempts to write the Control Change to the Bridge until a `processed` acknowledgment is received from the Bridge. If number of attempts exceeds `retries` then the Control Change Manager updates the Control Change key `state` with the value `failed`.

timeout.description: Time in seconds the Control Change Manager will wait after sending the Control Change for the Bridge to send a `processed` acknowledgement reply before retry sending the Control Change.

type.description: The type of the action. This is used to identify the action in the system.

type_title.description: The type name of the action.

title.description: The title of the action. This is a short description of the action that will be displayed to the user.

description.example: Beam Pump GW-B12-037 needs to be diagnosed.
description.description: The description of the action. This is a longer description of the action that will be displayed to the user.

expiration_date.description: The expiration date of the action. The action will be automatically cancelled if it is not completed by this date.

payload.description: The payload is a JSON object that contains the necessary information for the executor to perform the action. The structure of the payload will depend on the action type.

id.description: A unique randomly generated UUID as the key `id` for the Recommendation.

type_title.description: The Recommendation Type `title` associated with the Recommendation.

created.description: UTC time when the Recommendation was created, formatted in RFC 3339.

source.description: The process that created this Recommendation. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.

updated.description: UTC time when any Recommendation keys were last updated, formatted in RFC 3339.

updated_by.description: The process that last updated this Recommendation. This can be a user or an automated process like a workload, application, etc.
